public int countDigitOne(int n) {
    if (n < 0) return 0;
    int count = 0;
    long i = 1;
    while (i <= n) {
        long high = n / (i * 10);
        int current = (n / (int)i) % 10;
        long low = n % i;
        if (current > 1) {
            count += (high + 1) * i;
        } else if (current == 1) {
            count += high * i + (int)low + 1;
        } else {
            count += high * i;
        }
        i *= 10;
    }
    return count;
}

solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public int countDigitOne(int n) {
        if (n < 0) return 0;
        int count = 0;
        long i = 1;
        while (i <= n) {
            long divisor = i * 10;
            long high = n / divisor;
            int current = (n / (int)i) % 10;
            long low = n % i;
            if (current > 1) {
                count += (high + 1) * (int)i;
            } else if (current == 1) {
                count += high * (int)i + (int)low + 1;
            } else {
                count += high * (int)i;
            }
            i *= 10;
        }
        return count;
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(log n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution calculates the number of 1s in each digit position (units, tens, hundreds, etc.) using positional analysis. For each digit position, it determines how many times 1 appears based on higher digits, the current digit, and lower digits. This approach avoids iterating through all numbers up to n, achieving logarithmic time complexity. The key strength is its efficiency for large n, while the only weakness is the complexity of understanding the positional logic.
